Address Springfield Durban PSEA) “NariTM Morning Market Cafe SQUATTERS MARKET (DURBAN) [SUISIUARY OF RAMAWIARS TRADING CUNTHE) The only Cafe in the Market catering for refreshments and meals a la carte Phone 27937 Ramawtar's Continental Delicacies (SUBSOIARY OF RAMAWTARS TRADING CEN) Noted for its Varieties 171 Warwick Avenue Durban |THE SOUTH AFRICAN INDIAN WHO'S WHO FOREWORD Its, indeed, a privilege to me 0 be invited to write a Foreword by way of introducing to the public the third edition of the South Afican Tadian Who's Perhaps this invitation by the Board of Management of the publication is due 10 the fact tha 1 was long associated with the eitor and compile, the late Mr. Dhanee Bramdaw, who brought out the first publication of its kind in South Africa inthe year 1938. 1 well remember that when designing the first publication the late Me. Bramdaw had frequent consultations with me as t0 the scope and the material and general public information which were to include in it I may be said that Mr, Bramdaw being a journalist of wide experience knew his job of compiling and presenting & publication which would prove useful and table to the public and also presentable from its get-up. ‘The first publication ply proved all these combined efforts and the cover design and inclusion of several photographs were beautifully done, | know of my personal knowledge that the late Mr. Bramdaw used meticulous care in planning & Venture and in executing and in completing it in praiseworthy I know, also, he was complimented by both ‘Europeans and Indians on his fit effort ‘The South ‘African indian Wh's Who as 2 reference book of Indian People of South Africa has been used by the Government of India and also ‘thr oficial sources, a wel asthe public peneraly. ‘The present third eition brings the Who's Who uptodate. 1¢ contains information about Indians who areprominent and whose combined conibotons tothe varios activites to promote the moral, soc, cational, cultray religous ind politcal advancement of the community wil form a record of achievements ‘of wich any Community maybe proud 1 do hope and trast that this publication will yet matk another milestone in the Geldof progress snd general advancement stated by the Indian Community of South Altea. As South Africans we ae prowd of tis publication, 1 commend this third edition of the South Aftican Indian Who's Who to the general public and I am sure that people will not only find it useful but interesting find informative in all respects, (S. R. NAIDOO) Pictermaritzburg. ‘Tih Suly, 1958, a

pages.text: = THE SOUTH AFRICAN INDIAN WHO'S WHO Ta the visitors we extend our handshake ofgoodwill and hope your stay will be a pleasant one... Goodwill Lounge RESTAURANT & RESIDENCE 38 VICTORIA STREET — DURBAN Telephone 22780 “EXCELLENT CUISINE” THE SOUTH AFRICAN INDIAN WHOS WHO PREFACE ‘THE third edition of the South African Indian Who's Who is now presented to the public. Since its inception and the publication of the first ‘edition it has fulfilled the purposes for which it was intended. namely. to provide a documentary record cof the achievements of Indians in the Union of South Africa and of those engaged in trade, commerce and other professions. ‘The demand for such a publication thas not been confined to Indians only but also includes Europeans who have commercial and other business contacts wwith the Business Houses of the Indian Community. Further, its circulation reaches far beyond the limits of the Union. A large number of copies of this publication goes outside the Union of South Arica, thus along a seady referent in ascertaining information about public, commercial and professional personalities, ‘This issue will show what great strides the Indians within the Union have ‘made in all matters connected with religious, educational, social and sports matters the life of the Indian community. Compared with other Communities elsewhere is progress and evidence of self-expression and development and advancement in a relatively short period, despite the many handicaps and hardships they have hhad to endure, place them second to none in compatison with any other Com- ‘munity similarly placed elsewhere. Me. DDhance Bramdaw who began this Issue of the Who's Who suddenly met a tiagic end on the ath July 1982, while on a voyage to England. He was then compiling this thd edition, but his end, regretted by'a large circle of fiends and the publi, interrupted its publication for some time ‘This Issue is dedicated 10 hhis memory for his devoted and self-sacrificing labours in the interests of his people. I desire to place on record my sincere thanks to all. who assisted me in the compilation of this Edition, Their unstinted support and help have enabled me Jn a great measure to produce this publication. Bramdaw Publications, Natal, S.A. 2s
